The genus Anatherum is a group of tropical and subtropical grasses that are native to the Americas. Anatherum squarrosum is specifically known to be native to South America. The classification of Anatherum squarrosum is further defined by its botanical name, which follows the binomial nomenclature system used in taxonomy.

intro: Anatherum squarrosum, also known as Indian rice grass or Indian rice grass, is a type of herbaceous plant native to tropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities, offering health benefits such as wound healing, antimicrobial protection, and potential anti-cancer properties.
